title: Heavy and large goods vehicle drivers
description: Operate equipment used for applying concrete, asphalt, or other materials to road beds, parking lots, or airport runways and taxiways or for tamping gravel, dirt, or other materials. Includes concrete and asphalt paving machine operators, form tampers, tamping machine operators, and stone spreader operators. Operate pile drivers mounted on skids, barges, crawler treads, or locomotive cranes to drive pilings for retaining walls, bulkheads, and foundations of structures such as buildings, bridges, and piers. Operate one or several types of power construction equipment, such as motor graders, bulldozers, scrapers, compressors, pumps, derricks, shovels, tractors, or front-end loaders to excavate, move, and grade earth, erect structures, or pour concrete or other hard surface pavement. May repair and maintain equipment in addition to other duties. (Based on 14 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Inspect vehicles and check gas, oil, and water levels prior to departure.
Drive gasoline, diesel, or electrically powered multi-passenger vehicles to transport students between neighborhoods, schools, and activities.
Maintain order among students during trips to ensure safety and regulate vehicle speed and time spent at each stop.
Drive vehicles over specified routes or to specified destinations according to time schedules, complying with traffic regulations for a smooth ride.
Drive trucks with capacities greater than 13 tons, including tractor-trailer combinations, to transport and deliver various materials.
Load and unload trucks or help others with this using special loading-related equipment as necessary.
Maintain logs of working hours, vehicle service or repair status, following applicable central and devolved government regulations.
Check all load-related documentation and inspect loads to ensure that cargo is secure before transport.
Greet passengers, provide information on fares, schedules, and routes, and assist individuals with special needs on and off vehicles.
Report delays, accidents, and vehicle defects, using radios or by notifying dispatchers or company mechanics.
Operate equipment such as trucks, bulldozers, and other heavy machinery to perform construction tasks including excavation and transportation of materials.
Operate machinery to backfill excavations, and monitor health and safety standards on site.
Conduct pre-operational checks on equipment and perform minor repairs to ensure all machinery is in working order.
Follow relevant safety regulations and state laws governing vehicle operation, ensuring compliance during all tasks.
Create or maintain inclines, ramps, and handle site cleanings or maintenance using appropriate machinery.
Check the condition of a vehicle's tires, brakes, windshield wipers, lights, oil, fuel, water, and safety equipment to ensure that everything is in working order.
Perform routine vehicle maintenance, such as regulating tire pressure and adding gasoline, oil, and water, and report any vehicle malfunctions or needed repairs.
Communicate with dispatchers and confer with customers, supervisors, contractors, or other personnel to exchange information or to resolve problems.
Prepare, compile, and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or work-related accidents, including time, materials, expenditures, or crew activities.
Drive vehicles or operate machines or equipment to complete work assignments, assisting workers and performing tasks such as loading vehicles.
Monitor field work to ensure proper performance and use of materials, enforce safety rules and regulations, and inspect work areas for cleanliness or maintenance.
Perform or schedule repairs or preventive maintenance of vehicles or other equipment and engage in minor repairs as necessary.
Pick up and drop off passengers at scheduled locations, meet requests, and assist passengers entering and exiting vehicles.
Record vehicle routes and read maps or follow directions, while also regulating heating, lighting, and ventilation for passenger comfort.
Inspect or test materials, stock, vehicles, equipment, or facilities to ensure safety and compliance with specifications, and examine cargo to determine handling requirements.
